_note: ComfyUI API-format graph for Qwen-Image-Layered (4 RGBA layers). Qwen-Image-Layered decomposes ONE input image into N stacked RGBA layers; the model treats the N layers as a short 'video' along the temporal axis, so EmptyHunyuanLatentVideo.length encodes the layer count via length = 5 + (N-1)*4  (4 layers -> 17). REQUIRED on the ComfyUI box: (1) update ComfyUI to a build with native Qwen-Image nodes (UNETLoader qwen_image weight type, CLIPLoader type 'qwen_image', ModelSamplingAuraFlow, ReferenceLatent, EmptyHunyuanLatentVideo, LatentCutToBatch, VAEDecode with RGBA out); (2) models from HF 'Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image-Layered_ComfyUI' -> diffusion_models/qwen_image_layered_bf16.safetensors (or fp8mixed) and vae/qwen_image_layered_vae.safetensors; (3) text encoder qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors in models/text_encoders. Optional helper node pack: benjiyaya/ComfyUI-QwenImageLayeredToLength ('QwenImageLayered - Layer to Length Mapper') to compute length from a layer count instead of the hardcoded 17. qwen_worker.py sets node 5 (LoadImage) image, node 11 length, and reads PNGs from node 15 (SaveImage). If node names differ on your build, this file is the thing to edit.
